nodes.name: 地中海
nodes.summary: 地中海是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联18种鸟类，包括大红鹳、小凤头燕鸥、細嘴鷗等18种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 潜鸟科

nodes.name: LC
nodes.summary: LC表示 IUCN 无危等级，当前关联8208种鸟类，如鸵鸟、鸸鹋、南方鶴鴕等8208种鸟类。

nodes.name: 加泰罗尼亚
nodes.summary: 加泰罗尼亚是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括普通潜鸟，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 马里兰州

nodes.name: 索諾拉州
nodes.summary: 索諾拉州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联28种鸟类，包括Gambel's quail、Elegant quail、Clark's grebe等28种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 塔毛利帕斯州

nodes.name: 太平洋
nodes.summary: 太平洋是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联49种鸟类，包括针尾鸭、马岛冢雉、斑肩姬地鸠等49种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 得克萨斯州
nodes.summary: 得克萨斯州是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联37种鸟类，包括黑嘴天鹅、疣鼻棲鴨、蓝翅鸭等37种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 猎捕压力
nodes.summary: 直接猎捕或误捕造成额外死亡。

nodes.name: 潜鸟目
nodes.summary: 潜鸟目（Gaviiformes）是图谱中的目级分类节点，当前关联5种鸟类，如红喉潜鸟、普通潜鸟、白嘴潜鸟等5种鸟类。

nodes.name: 栖息地破坏
nodes.summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。

nodes.name: 普通潜鸟
nodes.summary: 普通潜鸟（Common loon，学名 Gavia immer），分类上属于潜鸟目，并归入潜鸟科，在图谱中标记为无危（LC）物种，已关联15个分布地点节点，主要威胁包括栖息地破坏、猎捕压力。

nodes.name: 莫罗湾 (加利福尼亚州)
nodes.summary: 莫罗湾 (加利福尼亚州)是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括普通潜鸟，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

nodes.name: 阿斯图里亚斯
nodes.summary: 阿斯图里亚斯是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联1种鸟类，包括普通潜鸟，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

links.label: 分布于
links.evidence: In North America, they winter mainly along north Atlantic and north-east Pacific coasts, many stopping off on the Great Lakes during their migration. They migrate in the day, starting about two hours after sunrise and flying at altitudes of 1500 to 2700 m above sea level, above the convective and turbulent layer of air. In winter they can be seen on North America coasts as far south as Baja California, Sonora, northern Sinaloa, southern Texas, and rarely northern Tamaulipas. In the east, several thousand winter along western European coasts, probably originating from Iceland, Greenland, and Canada. Their range extends into northwestern Europe from Finland to Portugal and southern and northwestern Spain (Galicia and Asturias), as well as the western Mediterranean off Catalonia, and off Morocco in Africa, although only a few hundred travel as far south as Iberia. Although wintering site fidelity is not well known, annually, adults are observed to return to the same wintering locations in the Pacific Ocean (Morro Bay), the Gulf of Mexico (Barataria Bay), the Atlantic Ocean (Maryland and Massachusetts), and the reservoir Lake Pateros. They usually winter along coasts and on inland lakes, bays, inlets, and streams, with birds migrating to the nearest body of water that will not freeze over in the winter: western Canadian loons go to the Pacific, Great Lakes loons to the Gulf of Mexico region, eastern Canadian loons to the Atlantic, and some loons to large inland lakes and reservoirs. They appear in most of the inland waters of the United States

links.label: 保护等级
links.evidence: status = LC

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: The United States Forest Service has designated the common loon a species of special status because of threats from habitat loss and toxic metal poisoning in its US range

links.label: 受威胁于
links.evidence: | last3=Bond | first3=Della | title=Mercury and selenium accumulation in common loons (Gavia immer) and common mergansers (Mergus merganser) from Eastern Canada | journal=Environmental Toxicology and Chemistry | volume=17 | issue=2 | pages=197–201| year=1998 | doi=10.1002/etc.5620170209 | bibcode=1998EnvTC..17..197S | s2cid=85336567 }} males being affected more because they eat larger fish. The common loon has also faced a decline in breeding range due to hunting, predation, and water-level fluctuations, or flooding
